title: rubus conduplicatus
intro: Rubus conduplicatus, also known as the double-leaf dewberry, is a type of shrub that belongs to the rosaceae family and is native to the southeastern United States. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus conduplicatus include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive health. The herbal preparations of Rubus conduplicatus include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be made by steeping the leaves, stems, and roots of the plant in hot water or a solvent.
